cards.front: What does 8 hours of metric space compactness imply?
Is a space with 8 hours of metric space compactness always complete?
cards.back: No, it may or may not be complete

cards.front: What is a common example of a space with 8 hours of metric space compactness?
cards.back: The closed interval [0, 1] with the standard metric

cards.front: Is 8 hours of metric space compactness sufficient for a space to be normal?
cards.back: No, it is not sufficient on its own

cards.front: What is the relationship between 8 hours of metric space compactness and the Heine-Borel theorem?
cards.back: 8 hours of metric space compactness is equivalent to the Heine-Borel theorem
